content: Vitamin A toxicity can occur when accumulated in the body beyond a certain threshold.
Toxicity typically requires more than 10,000 IU of vitamin A daily over months to years for a 150-pound adult.
Lab tests showing elevated serum vitamin A, gamma-glutamyl transferase, triglycerlycerides, alkaline phosphatase, prothrombin time, and cholesterol are indicative of vitamin A toxicity.
Excess vitamin A can worsen osteopenia and osteoporosis when deficient in calcium and vitamin D.
